rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source URL + type label — mandatory (scope: maintainer-block only, not student-facing body).
rules_evaluated.notes: Source-type labels ([practitioner direct]) live in maintainer-block Frameworks-riffed-on section (lines 128–129) — correct placement per 2026-05-03-research-source-type-labels-maintainer-only.md. Student-facing body at line 55 cites Cherny in builder voice without a label, which is the correct form. Klaassen and Huryn entries in maintainer block carry labels but no URLs; non-blocking (not student-facing body, and both have a source-verification TODO in the maintainer block). The '10% misrepresentation' heuristic (lines 13 and 38) is framed as an instructional directive ('Assume 10%'), not a research claim or statistic. No label or URL required for a teaching heuristic presented in directive voice.

Cherny video (May 2025) sits outside the 6-month freshness window. Exception is explicitly logged in maintainer block: 'Kept outside the 6-month window by decision; pedagogy match unbeaten by successors.' §2 permits exception with documented rationale. PASS.

No round-number AI failure/success statistics (95%/85%/80% zombie shapes) in body. The '10% misrepresentation' heuristic is a teaching directive ('Assume 10%'), not a stat cited as evidence. Not a zombie stat by definition (zombie stats are cited as research evidence; this is an instructional assumption). PASS.

Satisfies all four §11 requirements. The Klaassen and Huryn entries in Frameworks-riffed-on have no URL — both are flagged in existing todos_count.
